attack_chain.technique_name: Exploit Public-Facing Application / Internal Network Compromise
attack_chain.description: Threat actors gained an initial foothold by compromising the client's internal (on-premises) network. The specific initial access vector is not detailed in the source but is described as a sophisticated intrusion lifecycle.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actors compromised the client's own internal network

attack_chain.description: Attackers recovered cryptographic keys and cloud credentials from both on-premises and cloud systems, enabling access to the AWS environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: recovering the keys from both ends of the encryption

attack_chain.description: Using harvested credentials and keys, threat actors laterally moved from the compromised internal network into the AWS cloud environment.
Attackers used compromised cloud accounts and established backdoors to maintain persistent access to the AWS environment over multiple years.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: They persisted in the environment for a number of years, observing, gathering and exfiltrating over time

attack_chain.description: The threat actors created or maintained footholds, compromised accounts, and backdoors to facilitate re-entry and sustained access across the global enterprise.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: identified footholds, compromised accounts and back doors

attack_chain.description: Over time, attackers quietly traversed the environment, observing infrastructure and identifying data of interest before exfiltration.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: quietly entering, traversing and exfiltrating information from the global enterprise

attack_chain.description: Attackers collected data from the cloud environment, gathering information over an extended period.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: observing, gathering and exfiltrating over time

attack_chain.description: Sensitive data was exfiltrated from the global enterprise over an extended period. The method caused identifiable cost spikes in AWS billing.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: quietly entering, traversing and exfiltrating information from the global enterprise

initial_access_vector: Internal on-premises network compromised; threat actors then laterally moved into the AWS cloud environment using harvested credentials and encryption keys
persistence_mechanism: Compromised cloud accounts, backdoors, and footholds maintained for multiple years across the global enterprise
privilege_escalation: Credential theft (key recovery from both ends of encryption) used to access cloud resources; over-permissioned accounts likely leveraged (inferred from pattern described across cases)
